Print of Totoya Hokkei (1822) File:Daijin Takenouchino Sukune.jpg|Daijin Takenouchi no Sukune by [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]] ==External links== * Encyclopedia of [[Shinto]]: [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=548 Biographical note] * [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]]: [http://www.sinister-designs.com/graphicarts/takenouchi2.html Ukiyo-e image (1883)] * Nippon Kindai Banknote:[http://kjp-banknote.zening.info/en/yen1a.htm Banknote portrait (1916)] * Takenouchi no Sukune Meets Dragon King of the Sea, Dallas Museum of Art, bronze sculpture:[https://web.archive.org/web/20110725215318/http://dallasmuseumofart.org:8080/emuseum/media/view/Objects/3145828/406?t:state:flow=e8345f50-b33b-4bf9-83c8-e226a8426160] ==Notes== {{notelist}} == References ==]] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{Hachiman Faith}} [[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{Commons category|Takenouchi no Sukune}} [[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]] [[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[ja:武内宿禰]] [[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]] [[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]] [[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[zh:武內宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q1073668}} [[Category:84 births]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Hachiman faith]] [[Category:Japanese mythology|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto kami|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1073668]] Wikidata name: Takenouchi no Sukune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Legendary Japanese hero-statesman}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Takenouchi no Sukune
{{lang|ja|武内宿禰}} | image = Takeshiuchi Sukune.jpg | office = ''[[Omi (title)|Ōomi]]'' | caption = Takenouchi no Sukune drawn by [[:en:Kikuchi Yosai|Kikuchi Yosai]] | birth_date = 84 AD | death_date = Unknown | children = {{Plainlist| * [[Hata no Yashiro]] * [[Kose no Okara]] * [[Soga no Ishikawa]] * Heguri no Tsuku * [[Ki no Tsuno]] * Kume no Matio-hime * Nonoiro-hime * [[Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko]] * Wakugo no Sukune}} | father = {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Takenouchi no Sukune'''|武内宿禰}} or '''Takeshiuchi no Sukune''' was a legendary Japanese hero-statesman of the 1st century, and a [[Shinto]] [[kami]]. He is recorded in Japan's earliest literary texts, the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (ca. {{ill|712|en|712|WD=Q23521|lt=712}}) and the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' ({{ill|720|en|720|WD=Q23539|lt=720}}). == Life == Takenouchi no Sukune was supposedly the son of Princess Kagehime, and is said to be grandson to {{Nihongo|Prince Hikofutsuoshinomakoto|彦太忍信命}}. Descended from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}, Takenouchi no Sukune served under five legendary emperors, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}, but was perhaps best known for his service as Grand Minister (Ōomi) to the Regent {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, with whom he supposedly invaded Korea. While Jingu was regent to her son, Ojin, Takenouchi was accused of treason. He underwent the "{{ill|Trial by ordeal|en|Trial by ordeal|WD=Q40745|lt=ordeal}} of boiling water" as a way to prove his innocence. In addition to his martial services to these emperors, he was reputedly also a ''saniwa'', or {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=spirit medium}}. He is said to have been the grandson of HIkofutsuoshinomakoto in the Nihon Shoki, where as the Kojiki states that he was the son of Hikofutsuoshinomakoto.{{Cite web |title=Takeuchi no Sukune • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/takeuchi-no-sukune/ |access-date=2022-11-02 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} == Possible connections to Korea == Japanese historians have theorized a possible inference to Takenouchi no Sukune found in ancient Korea's ''[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]]'' where a name called "Udojugun (于道朱君/우도주군)" is mentioned.{{Cite book |title=[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]] |chapter=Chapter 45 - Battle of Seok Uro}} In the book, it states that during the time a Japanese envoy "Galnago (葛那古/갈나고)" stayed in {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, a royal named "Seok Uro (昔于老/석우로)" accompanied him. However after a while, the two started to banter where Seok Uro threatened to "make the emperor of Japan a salt making slave and his wife who cooks rice" as a heavy joke. After relaying the message to the emperor himself (referred to as Emperor Ookimi[?]/大王/オオキミ), a general named Udojugun was sent to the Korean peninsula and declared war against Silla. According to scholars, Udojugun and Takenouchi no Sukune might have been the same individual as Takenouchi no Sukune's pronunciation would have been closer to "Utusukune (内宿禰)" in {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese}}. In addition, ''{{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'' also mentions Takenouchi no Sukune's accomplishments during the Silla-Wa War (新羅・倭戦争) further adding credence to his involvement during the Silla period.{{Cite book |title={{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}} |volume=13 |chapter=異國人物 - 神功皇后征三韓}} As a similar sounding name appears in Korean records while also having historical documents that support his authenticity to an extent, scholars deduced that though not completely accurate, Takenouchi no Sukune can be considered an individual who had truly existed. According to world-renowned linguist and Japanese language expert {{ill|Alexander Vovin|en|Alexander Vovin|WD=Q848595|lt=Alexander Vovin}}, due to [[Amenohiboko]] being of Korean origin, Empress Jingū and her son and successor, Emperor Ōjin might have been native speakers of the {{ill|Korean language|en|Korean language|WD=Q9176|lt=Korean language}}.{{Cite book |last=Vovin |first=Alexander |title=Immigrants or Overlords? Korean Influences on Japan in the Archaic Period: a Linguistic Perspective |date=2012-06-14 |publisher=Institut für Kulturund Geitestesgeschichte Asiens |pages=29 |language=en}} Vovin states that since Takenouchi no Sukune was able to interact with Korean kingdoms during their rule, he stated that Takenouchi no Sukune may also have been of foreign origin as not only did he have jurisdiction over Japan-Korean affairs, but was also the father of many clan founders that have heavy connections with Korea. == Legacy == Twenty-eight Japanese clans are said to be descended from Takenouchi no Sukune, including {{ill|Takeuchi|en|Takeuchi|WD=Q23301551|lt=Takeuchi}} and [[Soga clan|Soga]]. He is a legendary figure, and is said to have drunk daily from a sacred well, which helped him live to be 280 years old. He is enshrined as a [[Kami]] at the [[Ube Shrine|Ube shrine]], in {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=the Iwami district}} of the {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} and at local [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman shrines]]. His portrait has appeared on the {{ill|Japanese yen|en|Japanese yen|WD=Q8146|lt=Japanese yen}}, and dolls of him are popular [[:en:Kodomo no hi|Children's Day]] gifts. [[File:Takeuchi1JPY.JPG|thumb|Yen bill with a representative drawing attributed to Takenouchi no Sukune on its obverse.]] *First convertible silver yen bill (1889-1958) *First five yen bill (1899-1939) *Third five yen bill (1916-1939) *Second one-yen bill (1943-1958) *Second 200 yen bill (1945-1946) ==Family== * '''Father:''' {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) * '''Mother:''' Yamashita no Kage-hime (影媛), sister of Kiinokuni no Miyatsukuko Uzuhiko (Ujihiko) (山下影日売) ** '''Wife(s):''' unknown *** '''Son:''' [[Hata no Yashiro]] (羽田矢代, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Hata clan (Ason)|Hata clan]] (波多氏) related to {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|WD=Q987290|lt=Hata clan}} (秦氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Kose no Okara]] (許勢小柄, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kose clan]] (巨勢氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Soga no Ishikawa]] (蘇我石川, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Soga clan]] (蘇我氏). *** '''Son:''' {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|WD=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tsuku}} (平群木菟, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Heguri clan]] (平群氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Ki no Tsuno]] (紀角, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ki clan]] (紀氏). *** '''Daughter:''' Kume no Matio-hime (久米能摩伊刀比売, ?–?) *** '''Daughter:''' Nonoiro-hime (怒能伊呂比売, ?–?) *** '''Son:''' [[Kazuragi no Sotsuhiko]] (葛城襲津彦, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Katsuragi clan]] (葛城氏). *** '''Son:''' Wakugo no Sukune (若子宿禰) ==Family tree== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Artwork== File:Empress Jingu and Minister Takenouchi by Utagawa Kunisada I.jpg|Empress Jingū (above) and Takenouchi no Sukune (below) Fishing at Chikuzen File:Takeuchi and the infant emperor.jpg|Minister Takeuchi carrying the infant {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}.
Made by [[Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] File:Empress Jingu and Takenouchi no Sukune Fishing at Chikuzen LACMA M.84.31.260.jpg|Empress Consort Jingū and Takenouchi fish in {{ill|Chikuzen Province |en|Chikuzen Province |WD=Q1047664|lt= Chikuzen}}.
Print of Tsukioka Yoshitoshi (1876) File:De regent Takeuchi no Sukune-Rijksmuseum RP-P-1958-369.jpeg|The Regent Takeuchi no Sukune.
Print of Totoya Hokkei (1822) File:Daijin Takenouchino Sukune.jpg|Daijin Takenouchi no Sukune by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}} ==External links== * Encyclopedia of [[Shinto]]: [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=548 Biographical note] * {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}}: [http://www.sinister-designs.com/graphicarts/takenouchi2.html Ukiyo-e image (1883)] * Nippon Kindai Banknote:[http://kjp-banknote.zening.info/en/yen1a.htm Banknote portrait (1916)] * Takenouchi no Sukune Meets Dragon King of the Sea, Dallas Museum of Art, bronze sculpture:[https://web.archive.org/web/20110725215318/http://dallasmuseumofart.org:8080/emuseum/media/view/Objects/3145828/406?t:state:flow=e8345f50-b33b-4bf9-83c8-e226a8426160] ==Notes== {{notelist}} == References ==]] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{Hachiman Faith}} [[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{Commons category|Takenouchi no Sukune}} [[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]] [[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[ja:武内宿禰]] [[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]] [[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]] [[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[zh:武內宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q1073668}} [[Category:84 births]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Hachiman faith]] [[Category:Japanese mythology|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto kami|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

He is enshrined as a [[Kami]] at the [[Ube Shrine|Ube shrine]], in {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=the Iwami district}} of the {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} and at local [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman shrines]]. His portrait has appeared on the {{ill|Japanese yen|en|Japanese yen|WD=Q8146|lt=Japanese yen}}, and dolls of him are popular [[:en:Kodomo no hi|Children's Day]] gifts. [[File:Takeuchi1JPY.JPG|thumb|Yen bill with a representative drawing attributed to Takenouchi no Sukune on its obverse.]] *First convertible silver yen bill (1889-1958) *First five yen bill (1899-1939) *Third five yen bill (1916-1939) *Second one-yen bill (1943-1958) *Second 200 yen bill (1945-1946) ==Family== * '''Father:''' {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) * '''Mother:''' Yamashita no Kage-hime (影媛), sister of Kiinokuni no Miyatsukuko Uzuhiko (Ujihiko) (山下影日売) ** '''Wife(s):''' unknown *** '''Son:''' [[Hata no Yashiro]] (羽田矢代, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Hata clan (Ason)|Hata clan]] (波多氏) related to {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|WD=Q987290|lt=Hata clan}} (秦氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Kose no Okara]] (許勢小柄, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kose clan]] (巨勢氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Soga no Ishikawa]] (蘇我石川, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Soga clan]] (蘇我氏). *** '''Son:''' {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|WD=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tsuku}} (平群木菟, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Heguri clan]] (平群氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Ki no Tsuno]] (紀角, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ki clan]] (紀氏). *** '''Daughter:''' Kume no Matio-hime (久米能摩伊刀比売, ?–?) *** '''Daughter:''' Nonoiro-hime (怒能伊呂比売, ?–?) *** '''Son:''' [[Kazuragi no Sotsuhiko]] (葛城襲津彦, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Katsuragi clan]] (葛城氏). *** '''Son:''' Wakugo no Sukune (若子宿禰) ==Family tree== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Artwork== File:Empress Jingu and Minister Takenouchi by Utagawa Kunisada I.jpg|Empress Jingū (above) and Takenouchi no Sukune (below) Fishing at Chikuzen File:Takeuchi and the infant emperor.jpg|Minister Takeuchi carrying the infant {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}.

[[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1073668]] Wikidata name: Takenouchi no Sukune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Legendary Japanese hero-statesman}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Takenouchi no Sukune
{{lang|ja|武内宿禰}} | image = Takeshiuchi Sukune.jpg | office = ''[[Omi (title)|Ōomi]]'' | caption = Takenouchi no Sukune drawn by [[:en:Kikuchi Yosai|Kikuchi Yosai]] | birth_date = 84 AD | death_date = Unknown | children = {{Plainlist| * [[Hata no Yashiro]] * [[Kose no Okara]] * [[Soga no Ishikawa]] * Heguri no Tsuku * [[Ki no Tsuno]] * Kume no Matio-hime * Nonoiro-hime * [[Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko]] * Wakugo no Sukune}} | father = {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Takenouchi no Sukune'''|武内宿禰}} or '''Takeshiuchi no Sukune''' was a legendary Japanese hero-statesman of the 1st century, and a [[Shinto]] [[kami]]. He is recorded in Japan's earliest literary texts, the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (ca. {{ill|712|en|712|WD=Q23521|lt=712}}) and the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' ({{ill|720|en|720|WD=Q23539|lt=720}}). == Life == Takenouchi no Sukune was supposedly the son of Princess Kagehime, and is said to be grandson to {{Nihongo|Prince Hikofutsuoshinomakoto|彦太忍信命}}. Descended from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}, Takenouchi no Sukune served under five legendary emperors, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}, but was perhaps best known for his service as Grand Minister (Ōomi) to the Regent {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, with whom he supposedly invaded Korea. While Jingu was regent to her son, Ojin, Takenouchi was accused of treason. He underwent the "{{ill|Trial by ordeal|en|Trial by ordeal|WD=Q40745|lt=ordeal}} of boiling water" as a way to prove his innocence. In addition to his martial services to these emperors, he was reputedly also a ''saniwa'', or {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=spirit medium}}. He is said to have been the grandson of HIkofutsuoshinomakoto in the Nihon Shoki, where as the Kojiki states that he was the son of Hikofutsuoshinomakoto.{{Cite web |title=Takeuchi no Sukune • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/takeuchi-no-sukune/ |access-date=2022-11-02 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} == Possible connections to Korea == Japanese historians have theorized a possible inference to Takenouchi no Sukune found in ancient Korea's ''[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]]'' where a name called "Udojugun (于道朱君/우도주군)" is mentioned.{{Cite book |title=[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]] |chapter=Chapter 45 - Battle of Seok Uro}} In the book, it states that during the time a Japanese envoy "Galnago (葛那古/갈나고)" stayed in {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, a royal named "Seok Uro (昔于老/석우로)" accompanied him. However after a while, the two started to banter where Seok Uro threatened to "make the emperor of Japan a salt making slave and his wife who cooks rice" as a heavy joke. After relaying the message to the emperor himself (referred to as Emperor Ookimi[?]/大王/オオキミ), a general named Udojugun was sent to the Korean peninsula and declared war against Silla. According to scholars, Udojugun and Takenouchi no Sukune might have been the same individual as Takenouchi no Sukune's pronunciation would have been closer to "Utusukune (内宿禰)" in {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese}}. In addition, ''{{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'' also mentions Takenouchi no Sukune's accomplishments during the Silla-Wa War (新羅・倭戦争) further adding credence to his involvement during the Silla period.{{Cite book |title={{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}} |volume=13 |chapter=異國人物 - 神功皇后征三韓}} As a similar sounding name appears in Korean records while also having historical documents that support his authenticity to an extent, scholars deduced that though not completely accurate, Takenouchi no Sukune can be considered an individual who had truly existed. According to world-renowned linguist and Japanese language expert {{ill|Alexander Vovin|en|Alexander Vovin|WD=Q848595|lt=Alexander Vovin}}, due to [[Amenohiboko]] being of Korean origin, Empress Jingū and her son and successor, Emperor Ōjin might have been native speakers of the {{ill|Korean language|en|Korean language|WD=Q9176|lt=Korean language}}.{{Cite book |last=Vovin |first=Alexander |title=Immigrants or Overlords? Korean Influences on Japan in the Archaic Period: a Linguistic Perspective |date=2012-06-14 |publisher=Institut für Kulturund Geitestesgeschichte Asiens |pages=29 |language=en}} Vovin states that since Takenouchi no Sukune was able to interact with Korean kingdoms during their rule, he stated that Takenouchi no Sukune may also have been of foreign origin as not only did he have jurisdiction over Japan-Korean affairs, but was also the father of many clan founders that have heavy connections with Korea. == Legacy == Twenty-eight Japanese clans are said to be descended from Takenouchi no Sukune, including {{ill|Takeuchi|en|Takeuchi|WD=Q23301551|lt=Takeuchi}} and [[Soga clan|Soga]]. He is a legendary figure, and is said to have drunk daily from a sacred well, which helped him live to be 280 years old. He is enshrined as a [[Kami]] at the [[Ube Shrine|Ube shrine]], in {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=the Iwami district}} of the {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} and at local [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman shrines]]. His portrait has appeared on the {{ill|Japanese yen|en|Japanese yen|WD=Q8146|lt=Japanese yen}}, and dolls of him are popular [[:en:Kodomo no hi|Children's Day]] gifts. [[File:Takeuchi1JPY.JPG|thumb|Yen bill with a representative drawing attributed to Takenouchi no Sukune on its obverse.]] *First convertible silver yen bill (1889-1958) *First five yen bill (1899-1939) *Third five yen bill (1916-1939) *Second one-yen bill (1943-1958) *Second 200 yen bill (1945-1946) ==Family== * '''Father:''' {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) * '''Mother:''' Yamashita no Kage-hime (影媛), sister of Kiinokuni no Miyatsukuko Uzuhiko (Ujihiko) (山下影日売) ** '''Wife(s):''' unknown *** '''Son:''' [[Hata no Yashiro]] (羽田矢代, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Hata clan (Ason)|Hata clan]] (波多氏) related to {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|WD=Q987290|lt=Hata clan}} (秦氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Kose no Okara]] (許勢小柄, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kose clan]] (巨勢氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Soga no Ishikawa]] (蘇我石川, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Soga clan]] (蘇我氏). *** '''Son:''' {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|WD=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tsuku}} (平群木菟, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Heguri clan]] (平群氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Ki no Tsuno]] (紀角, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ki clan]] (紀氏). *** '''Daughter:''' Kume no Matio-hime (久米能摩伊刀比売, ?–?) *** '''Daughter:''' Nonoiro-hime (怒能伊呂比売, ?–?) *** '''Son:''' [[Kazuragi no Sotsuhiko]] (葛城襲津彦, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Katsuragi clan]] (葛城氏). *** '''Son:''' Wakugo no Sukune (若子宿禰) ==Family tree== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Artwork== File:Empress Jingu and Minister Takenouchi by Utagawa Kunisada I.jpg|Empress Jingū (above) and Takenouchi no Sukune (below) Fishing at Chikuzen File:Takeuchi and the infant emperor.jpg|Minister Takeuchi carrying the infant {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}.
Made by [[Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] File:Empress Jingu and Takenouchi no Sukune Fishing at Chikuzen LACMA M.84.31.260.jpg|Empress Consort Jingū and Takenouchi fish in {{ill|Chikuzen Province |en|Chikuzen Province |WD=Q1047664|lt= Chikuzen}}.

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/takeuchi-no-sukune/ |access-date=2022-11-02 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} == Possible connections to Korea == Japanese historians have theorized a possible inference to Takenouchi no Sukune found in ancient Korea's ''[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]]'' where a name called "Udojugun (于道朱君/우도주군)" is mentioned.{{Cite book |title=[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]] |chapter=Chapter 45 - Battle of Seok Uro}} In the book, it states that during the time a Japanese envoy "Galnago (葛那古/갈나고)" stayed in {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, a royal named "Seok Uro (昔于老/석우로)" accompanied him. After relaying the message to the emperor himself (referred to as Emperor Ookimi[?]/大王/オオキミ), a general named Udojugun was sent to the Korean peninsula and declared war against Silla. According to scholars, Udojugun and Takenouchi no Sukune might have been the same individual as Takenouchi no Sukune's pronunciation would have been closer to "Utusukune (内宿禰)" in {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese}}. In addition, ''{{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'' also mentions Takenouchi no Sukune's accomplishments during the Silla-Wa War (新羅・倭戦争) further adding credence to his involvement during the Silla period.{{Cite book |title={{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}} |volume=13 |chapter=異國人物 - 神功皇后征三韓}} As a similar sounding name appears in Korean records while also having historical documents that support his authenticity to an extent, scholars deduced that though not completely accurate, Takenouchi no Sukune can be considered an individual who had truly existed. According to world-renowned linguist and Japanese language expert {{ill|Alexander Vovin|en|Alexander Vovin|WD=Q848595|lt=Alexander Vovin}}, due to [[Amenohiboko]] being of Korean origin, Empress Jingū and her son and successor, Emperor Ōjin might have been native speakers of the {{ill|Korean language|en|Korean language|WD=Q9176|lt=Korean language}}.{{Cite book |last=Vovin |first=Alexander |title=Immigrants or Overlords? Korean Influences on Japan in the Archaic Period: a Linguistic Perspective |date=2012-06-14 |publisher=Institut für Kulturund Geitestesgeschichte Asiens |pages=29 |language=en}} Vovin states that since Takenouchi no Sukune was able to interact with Korean kingdoms during their rule, he stated that Takenouchi no Sukune may also have been of foreign origin as not only did he have jurisdiction over Japan-Korean affairs, but was also the father of many clan founders that have heavy connections with Korea. == Legacy == Twenty-eight Japanese clans are said to be descended from Takenouchi no Sukune, including {{ill|Takeuchi|en|Takeuchi|WD=Q23301551|lt=Takeuchi}} and [[Soga clan|Soga]]. He is a legendary figure, and is said to have drunk daily from a sacred well, which helped him live to be 280 years old. He is enshrined as a [[Kami]] at the [[Ube Shrine|Ube shrine]], in {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=the Iwami district}} of the {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} and at local [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman shrines]]. His portrait has appeared on the {{ill|Japanese yen|en|Japanese yen|WD=Q8146|lt=Japanese yen}}, and dolls of him are popular [[:en:Kodomo no hi|Children's Day]] gifts. [[File:Takeuchi1JPY.JPG|thumb|Yen bill with a representative drawing attributed to Takenouchi no Sukune on its obverse.]] *First convertible silver yen bill (1889-1958) *First five yen bill (1899-1939) *Third five yen bill (1916-1939) *Second one-yen bill (1943-1958) *Second 200 yen bill (1945-1946) ==Family== * '''Father:''' {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) * '''Mother:''' Yamashita no Kage-hime (影媛), sister of Kiinokuni no Miyatsukuko Uzuhiko (Ujihiko) (山下影日売) ** '''Wife(s):''' unknown *** '''Son:''' [[Hata no Yashiro]] (羽田矢代, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Hata clan (Ason)|Hata clan]] (波多氏) related to {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|WD=Q987290|lt=Hata clan}} (秦氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Kose no Okara]] (許勢小柄, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kose clan]] (巨勢氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Soga no Ishikawa]] (蘇我石川, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Soga clan]] (蘇我氏). *** '''Son:''' {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|WD=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tsuku}} (平群木菟, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Heguri clan]] (平群氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Ki no Tsuno]] (紀角, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ki clan]] (紀氏). *** '''Daughter:''' Kume no Matio-hime (久米能摩伊刀比売, ?–?) *** '''Daughter:''' Nonoiro-hime (怒能伊呂比売, ?–?) *** '''Son:''' [[Kazuragi no Sotsuhiko]] (葛城襲津彦, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Katsuragi clan]] (葛城氏). *** '''Son:''' Wakugo no Sukune (若子宿禰) ==Family tree== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Artwork== File:Empress Jingu and Minister Takenouchi by Utagawa Kunisada I.jpg|Empress Jingū (above) and Takenouchi no Sukune (below) Fishing at Chikuzen File:Takeuchi and the infant emperor.jpg|Minister Takeuchi carrying the infant {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}.
Made by [[Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] File:Empress Jingu and Takenouchi no Sukune Fishing at Chikuzen LACMA M.84.31.260.jpg|Empress Consort Jingū and Takenouchi fish in {{ill|Chikuzen Province |en|Chikuzen Province |WD=Q1047664|lt= Chikuzen}}.

Print of Totoya Hokkei (1822) File:Daijin Takenouchino Sukune.jpg|Daijin Takenouchi no Sukune by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}} ==External links== * Encyclopedia of [[Shinto]]: [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=548 Biographical note] * {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}}: [http://www.sinister-designs.com/graphicarts/takenouchi2.html Ukiyo-e image (1883)] * Nippon Kindai Banknote:[http://kjp-banknote.zening.info/en/yen1a.htm Banknote portrait (1916)] * Takenouchi no Sukune Meets Dragon King of the Sea, Dallas Museum of Art, bronze sculpture:[https://web.archive.org/web/20110725215318/http://dallasmuseumofart.org:8080/emuseum/media/view/Objects/3145828/406?t:state:flow=e8345f50-b33b-4bf9-83c8-e226a8426160] ==Notes== {{notelist}} == References ==]] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{Hachiman Faith}} [[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{Commons category|Takenouchi no Sukune}} [[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]] [[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[ja:武内宿禰]] [[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]] [[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]] [[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[zh:武內宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q1073668}} [[Category:84 births]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Hachiman faith]] [[Category:Japanese mythology|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Shinto kami|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1073668]] Wikidata name: Takenouchi no Sukune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Legendary Japanese hero-statesman}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Takenouchi no Sukune
{{lang|ja|武内宿禰}} | image = Takeshiuchi Sukune.jpg | office = ''[[Omi (title)|Ōomi]]'' | caption = Takenouchi no Sukune drawn by [[:en:Kikuchi Yosai|Kikuchi Yosai]] | birth_date = 84 AD | death_date = Unknown | children = {{Plainlist| * [[Hata no Yashiro]] * [[Kose no Okara]] * [[Soga no Ishikawa]] * Heguri no Tsuku * [[Ki no Tsuno]] * Kume no Matio-hime * Nonoiro-hime * [[Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko]] * Wakugo no Sukune}} | father = {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Takenouchi no Sukune'''|武内宿禰}} or '''Takeshiuchi no Sukune''' was a legendary Japanese hero-statesman of the 1st century, and a [[Shinto]] [[kami]]. He is recorded in Japan's earliest literary texts, the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (ca. {{ill|712|en|712|WD=Q23521|lt=712}}) and the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' ({{ill|720|en|720|WD=Q23539|lt=720}}). == Life == Takenouchi no Sukune was supposedly the son of Princess Kagehime, and is said to be grandson to {{Nihongo|Prince Hikofutsuoshinomakoto|彦太忍信命}}. Descended from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}, Takenouchi no Sukune served under five legendary emperors, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}, but was perhaps best known for his service as Grand Minister (Ōomi) to the Regent {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, with whom he supposedly invaded Korea. While Jingu was regent to her son, Ojin, Takenouchi was accused of treason. He underwent the "{{ill|Trial by ordeal|en|Trial by ordeal|WD=Q40745|lt=ordeal}} of boiling water" as a way to prove his innocence. In addition to his martial services to these emperors, he was reputedly also a ''saniwa'', or {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=spirit medium}}. He is said to have been the grandson of HIkofutsuoshinomakoto in the Nihon Shoki, where as the Kojiki states that he was the son of Hikofutsuoshinomakoto.{{Cite web |title=Takeuchi no Sukune • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/takeuchi-no-sukune/ |access-date=2022-11-02 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} == Possible connections to Korea == Japanese historians have theorized a possible inference to Takenouchi no Sukune found in ancient Korea's ''[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]]'' where a name called "Udojugun (于道朱君/우도주군)" is mentioned.{{Cite book |title=[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]] |chapter=Chapter 45 - Battle of Seok Uro}} In the book, it states that during the time a Japanese envoy "Galnago (葛那古/갈나고)" stayed in {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, a royal named "Seok Uro (昔于老/석우로)" accompanied him. However after a while, the two started to banter where Seok Uro threatened to "make the emperor of Japan a salt making slave and his wife who cooks rice" as a heavy joke. After relaying the message to the emperor himself (referred to as Emperor Ookimi[?]/大王/オオキミ), a general named Udojugun was sent to the Korean peninsula and declared war against Silla. According to scholars, Udojugun and Takenouchi no Sukune might have been the same individual as Takenouchi no Sukune's pronunciation would have been closer to "Utusukune (内宿禰)" in {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese}}. In addition, ''{{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'' also mentions Takenouchi no Sukune's accomplishments during the Silla-Wa War (新羅・倭戦争) further adding credence to his involvement during the Silla period.{{Cite book |title={{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}} |volume=13 |chapter=異國人物 - 神功皇后征三韓}} As a similar sounding name appears in Korean records while also having historical documents that support his authenticity to an extent, scholars deduced that though not completely accurate, Takenouchi no Sukune can be considered an individual who had truly existed. According to world-renowned linguist and Japanese language expert {{ill|Alexander Vovin|en|Alexander Vovin|WD=Q848595|lt=Alexander Vovin}}, due to [[Amenohiboko]] being of Korean origin, Empress Jingū and her son and successor, Emperor Ōjin might have been native speakers of the {{ill|Korean language|en|Korean language|WD=Q9176|lt=Korean language}}.{{Cite book |last=Vovin |first=Alexander |title=Immigrants or Overlords? Korean Influences on Japan in the Archaic Period: a Linguistic Perspective |date=2012-06-14 |publisher=Institut für Kulturund Geitestesgeschichte Asiens |pages=29 |language=en}} Vovin states that since Takenouchi no Sukune was able to interact with Korean kingdoms during their rule, he stated that Takenouchi no Sukune may also have been of foreign origin as not only did he have jurisdiction over Japan-Korean affairs, but was also the father of many clan founders that have heavy connections with Korea. == Legacy == Twenty-eight Japanese clans are said to be descended from Takenouchi no Sukune, including {{ill|Takeuchi|en|Takeuchi|WD=Q23301551|lt=Takeuchi}} and [[Soga clan|Soga]]. He is a legendary figure, and is said to have drunk daily from a sacred well, which helped him live to be 280 years old. He is enshrined as a [[Kami]] at the [[Ube Shrine|Ube shrine]], in {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=the Iwami district}} of the {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} and at local [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman shrines]]. His portrait has appeared on the {{ill|Japanese yen|en|Japanese yen|WD=Q8146|lt=Japanese yen}}, and dolls of him are popular [[:en:Kodomo no hi|Children's Day]] gifts. [[File:Takeuchi1JPY.JPG|thumb|Yen bill with a representative drawing attributed to Takenouchi no Sukune on its obverse.]] *First convertible silver yen bill (1889-1958) *First five yen bill (1899-1939) *Third five yen bill (1916-1939) *Second one-yen bill (1943-1958) *Second 200 yen bill (1945-1946) ==Family== * '''Father:''' {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) * '''Mother:''' Yamashita no Kage-hime (影媛), sister of Kiinokuni no Miyatsukuko Uzuhiko (Ujihiko) (山下影日売) ** '''Wife(s):''' unknown *** '''Son:''' [[Hata no Yashiro]] (羽田矢代, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Hata clan (Ason)|Hata clan]] (波多氏) related to {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|WD=Q987290|lt=Hata clan}} (秦氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Kose no Okara]] (許勢小柄, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kose clan]] (巨勢氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Soga no Ishikawa]] (蘇我石川, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Soga clan]] (蘇我氏). *** '''Son:''' {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|WD=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tsuku}} (平群木菟, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Heguri clan]] (平群氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Ki no Tsuno]] (紀角, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ki clan]] (紀氏). *** '''Daughter:''' Kume no Matio-hime (久米能摩伊刀比売, ?–?) *** '''Daughter:''' Nonoiro-hime (怒能伊呂比売, ?–?) *** '''Son:''' [[Kazuragi no Sotsuhiko]] (葛城襲津彦, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Katsuragi clan]] (葛城氏). *** '''Son:''' Wakugo no Sukune (若子宿禰) ==Family tree== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Artwork== File:Empress Jingu and Minister Takenouchi by Utagawa Kunisada I.jpg|Empress Jingū (above) and Takenouchi no Sukune (below) Fishing at Chikuzen File:Takeuchi and the infant emperor.jpg|Minister Takeuchi carrying the infant {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}.
Made by [[Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] File:Empress Jingu and Takenouchi no Sukune Fishing at Chikuzen LACMA M.84.31.260.jpg|Empress Consort Jingū and Takenouchi fish in {{ill|Chikuzen Province |en|Chikuzen Province |WD=Q1047664|lt= Chikuzen}}.

While Jingu was regent to her son, Ojin, Takenouchi was accused of treason. He underwent the "{{ill|Trial by ordeal|en|Trial by ordeal|WD=Q40745|lt=ordeal}} of boiling water" as a way to prove his innocence. In addition to his martial services to these emperors, he was reputedly also a ''saniwa'', or {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=spirit medium}}. He is said to have been the grandson of HIkofutsuoshinomakoto in the Nihon Shoki, where as the Kojiki states that he was the son of Hikofutsuoshinomakoto.{{Cite web |title=Takeuchi no Sukune • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/takeuchi-no-sukune/ |access-date=2022-11-02 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} == Possible connections to Korea == Japanese historians have theorized a possible inference to Takenouchi no Sukune found in ancient Korea's ''[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]]'' where a name called "Udojugun (于道朱君/우도주군)" is mentioned.{{Cite book |title=[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]] |chapter=Chapter 45 - Battle of Seok Uro}} In the book, it states that during the time a Japanese envoy "Galnago (葛那古/갈나고)" stayed in {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, a royal named "Seok Uro (昔于老/석우로)" accompanied him. However after a while, the two started to banter where Seok Uro threatened to "make the emperor of Japan a salt making slave and his wife who cooks rice" as a heavy joke. After relaying the message to the emperor himself (referred to as Emperor Ookimi[?]/大王/オオキミ), a general named Udojugun was sent to the Korean peninsula and declared war against Silla. According to scholars, Udojugun and Takenouchi no Sukune might have been the same individual as Takenouchi no Sukune's pronunciation would have been closer to "Utusukune (内宿禰)" in {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese}}. In addition, ''{{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'' also mentions Takenouchi no Sukune's accomplishments during the Silla-Wa War (新羅・倭戦争) further adding credence to his involvement during the Silla period.{{Cite book |title={{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}} |volume=13 |chapter=異國人物 - 神功皇后征三韓}} As a similar sounding name appears in Korean records while also having historical documents that support his authenticity to an extent, scholars deduced that though not completely accurate, Takenouchi no Sukune can be considered an individual who had truly existed. According to world-renowned linguist and Japanese language expert {{ill|Alexander Vovin|en|Alexander Vovin|WD=Q848595|lt=Alexander Vovin}}, due to [[Amenohiboko]] being of Korean origin, Empress Jingū and her son and successor, Emperor Ōjin might have been native speakers of the {{ill|Korean language|en|Korean language|WD=Q9176|lt=Korean language}}.{{Cite book |last=Vovin |first=Alexander |title=Immigrants or Overlords? Korean Influences on Japan in the Archaic Period: a Linguistic Perspective |date=2012-06-14 |publisher=Institut für Kulturund Geitestesgeschichte Asiens |pages=29 |language=en}} Vovin states that since Takenouchi no Sukune was able to interact with Korean kingdoms during their rule, he stated that Takenouchi no Sukune may also have been of foreign origin as not only did he have jurisdiction over Japan-Korean affairs, but was also the father of many clan founders that have heavy connections with Korea. == Legacy == Twenty-eight Japanese clans are said to be descended from Takenouchi no Sukune, including {{ill|Takeuchi|en|Takeuchi|WD=Q23301551|lt=Takeuchi}} and [[Soga clan|Soga]]. He is a legendary figure, and is said to have drunk daily from a sacred well, which helped him live to be 280 years old. He is enshrined as a [[Kami]] at the [[Ube Shrine|Ube shrine]], in {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=the Iwami district}} of the {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} and at local [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman shrines]]. His portrait has appeared on the {{ill|Japanese yen|en|Japanese yen|WD=Q8146|lt=Japanese yen}}, and dolls of him are popular [[:en:Kodomo no hi|Children's Day]] gifts. [[File:Takeuchi1JPY.JPG|thumb|Yen bill with a representative drawing attributed to Takenouchi no Sukune on its obverse.]] *First convertible silver yen bill (1889-1958) *First five yen bill (1899-1939) *Third five yen bill (1916-1939) *Second one-yen bill (1943-1958) *Second 200 yen bill (1945-1946) ==Family== * '''Father:''' {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) * '''Mother:''' Yamashita no Kage-hime (影媛), sister of Kiinokuni no Miyatsukuko Uzuhiko (Ujihiko) (山下影日売) ** '''Wife(s):''' unknown *** '''Son:''' [[Hata no Yashiro]] (羽田矢代, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Hata clan (Ason)|Hata clan]] (波多氏) related to {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|WD=Q987290|lt=Hata clan}} (秦氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Kose no Okara]] (許勢小柄, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kose clan]] (巨勢氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Soga no Ishikawa]] (蘇我石川, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Soga clan]] (蘇我氏). *** '''Son:''' {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|WD=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tsuku}} (平群木菟, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Heguri clan]] (平群氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Ki no Tsuno]] (紀角, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ki clan]] (紀氏). *** '''Daughter:''' Kume no Matio-hime (久米能摩伊刀比売, ?–?) *** '''Daughter:''' Nonoiro-hime (怒能伊呂比売, ?–?) *** '''Son:''' [[Kazuragi no Sotsuhiko]] (葛城襲津彦, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Katsuragi clan]] (葛城氏). *** '''Son:''' Wakugo no Sukune (若子宿禰) ==Family tree== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Artwork== File:Empress Jingu and Minister Takenouchi by Utagawa Kunisada I.jpg|Empress Jingū (above) and Takenouchi no Sukune (below) Fishing at Chikuzen File:Takeuchi and the infant emperor.jpg|Minister Takeuchi carrying the infant {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}.
Made by [[Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] File:Empress Jingu and Takenouchi no Sukune Fishing at Chikuzen LACMA M.84.31.260.jpg|Empress Consort Jingū and Takenouchi fish in {{ill|Chikuzen Province |en|Chikuzen Province |WD=Q1047664|lt= Chikuzen}}.
Print of Tsukioka Yoshitoshi (1876) File:De regent Takeuchi no Sukune-Rijksmuseum RP-P-1958-369.jpeg|The Regent Takeuchi no Sukune.
Print of Totoya Hokkei (1822) File:Daijin Takenouchino Sukune.jpg|Daijin Takenouchi no Sukune by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}} ==External links== * Encyclopedia of [[Shinto]]: [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=548 Biographical note] * {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}}: [http://www.sinister-designs.com/graphicarts/takenouchi2.html Ukiyo-e image (1883)] * Nippon Kindai Banknote:[http://kjp-banknote.zening.info/en/yen1a.htm Banknote portrait (1916)] * Takenouchi no Sukune Meets Dragon King of the Sea, Dallas Museum of Art, bronze sculpture:[https://web.archive.org/web/20110725215318/http://dallasmuseumofart.org:8080/emuseum/media/view/Objects/3145828/406?t:state:flow=e8345f50-b33b-4bf9-83c8-e226a8426160] ==Notes== {{notelist}} == References ==]] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{Hachiman Faith}} [[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{Commons category|Takenouchi no Sukune}} [[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]] [[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[ja:武内宿禰]] [[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]] [[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]] [[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[zh:武內宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q1073668}} [[Category:84 births]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Hachiman faith]] [[Category:Japanese mythology|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Shinto kami|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] {{Short description|Legendary Japanese hero-statesman}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Takenouchi no Sukune
{{lang|ja|武内宿禰}} | image = Takeshiuchi Sukune.jpg | office = ''[[Omi (title)|Ōomi]]'' | caption = Takenouchi no Sukune drawn by [[:en:Kikuchi Yosai|Kikuchi Yosai]] | birth_date = 84 AD | death_date = Unknown | children = {{Plainlist| * [[Hata no Yashiro]] * [[Kose no Okara]] * [[Soga no Ishikawa]] * Heguri no Tsuku * [[Ki no Tsuno]] * Kume no Matio-hime * Nonoiro-hime * [[Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko]] * Wakugo no Sukune}} | father = {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Takenouchi no Sukune'''|武内宿禰}} or '''Takeshiuchi no Sukune''' was a legendary Japanese hero-statesman of the 1st century, and a [[Shinto]] [[kami]]. He is recorded in Japan's earliest literary texts, the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (ca. {{ill|712|en|712|WD=Q23521|lt=712}}) and the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' ({{ill|720|en|720|WD=Q23539|lt=720}}). == Life == Takenouchi no Sukune was supposedly the son of Princess Kagehime, and is said to be grandson to {{Nihongo|Prince Hikofutsuoshinomakoto|彦太忍信命}}. Descended from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}, Takenouchi no Sukune served under five legendary emperors, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}, but was perhaps best known for his service as Grand Minister (Ōomi) to the Regent {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, with whom he supposedly invaded Korea. While Jingu was regent to her son, Ojin, Takenouchi was accused of treason. He underwent the "{{ill|Trial by ordeal|en|Trial by ordeal|WD=Q40745|lt=ordeal}} of boiling water" as a way to prove his innocence. In addition to his martial services to these emperors, he was reputedly also a ''saniwa'', or {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=spirit medium}}. He is said to have been the grandson of HIkofutsuoshinomakoto in the Nihon Shoki, where as the Kojiki states that he was the son of Hikofutsuoshinomakoto.{{Cite web |title=Takeuchi no Sukune • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/takeuchi-no-sukune/ |access-date=2022-11-02 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} == Possible connections to Korea == Japanese historians have theorized a possible inference to Takenouchi no Sukune found in ancient Korea's ''[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]]'' where a name called "Udojugun (于道朱君/우도주군)" is mentioned.{{Cite book |title=[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]] |chapter=Chapter 45 - Battle of Seok Uro}} In the book, it states that during the time a Japanese envoy "Galnago (葛那古/갈나고)" stayed in {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, a royal named "Seok Uro (昔于老/석우로)" accompanied him. However after a while, the two started to banter where Seok Uro threatened to "make the emperor of Japan a salt making slave and his wife who cooks rice" as a heavy joke. After relaying the message to the emperor himself (referred to as Emperor Ookimi[?]/大王/オオキミ), a general named Udojugun was sent to the Korean peninsula and declared war against Silla. According to scholars, Udojugun and Takenouchi no Sukune might have been the same individual as Takenouchi no Sukune's pronunciation would have been closer to "Utusukune (内宿禰)" in {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese}}. In addition, ''{{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'' also mentions Takenouchi no Sukune's accomplishments during the Silla-Wa War (新羅・倭戦争) further adding credence to his involvement during the Silla period.{{Cite book |title={{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}} |volume=13 |chapter=異國人物 - 神功皇后征三韓}} As a similar sounding name appears in Korean records while also having historical documents that support his authenticity to an extent, scholars deduced that though not completely accurate, Takenouchi no Sukune can be considered an individual who had truly existed. According to world-renowned linguist and Japanese language expert {{ill|Alexander Vovin|en|Alexander Vovin|WD=Q848595|lt=Alexander Vovin}}, due to [[Amenohiboko]] being of Korean origin, Empress Jingū and her son and successor, Emperor Ōjin might have been native speakers of the {{ill|Korean language|en|Korean language|WD=Q9176|lt=Korean language}}.{{Cite book |last=Vovin |first=Alexander |title=Immigrants or Overlords? Korean Influences on Japan in the Archaic Period: a Linguistic Perspective |date=2012-06-14 |publisher=Institut für Kulturund Geitestesgeschichte Asiens |pages=29 |language=en}} Vovin states that since Takenouchi no Sukune was able to interact with Korean kingdoms during their rule, he stated that Takenouchi no Sukune may also have been of foreign origin as not only did he have jurisdiction over Japan-Korean affairs, but was also the father of many clan founders that have heavy connections with Korea. == Legacy == Twenty-eight Japanese clans are said to be descended from Takenouchi no Sukune, including {{ill|Takeuchi|en|Takeuchi|WD=Q23301551|lt=Takeuchi}} and [[Soga clan|Soga]]. He is a legendary figure, and is said to have drunk daily from a sacred well, which helped him live to be 280 years old. He is enshrined as a [[Kami]] at the [[Ube Shrine|Ube shrine]], in {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=the Iwami district}} of the {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} and at local [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman shrines]]. His portrait has appeared on the {{ill|Japanese yen|en|Japanese yen|WD=Q8146|lt=Japanese yen}}, and dolls of him are popular [[:en:Kodomo no hi|Children's Day]] gifts. [[File:Takeuchi1JPY.JPG|thumb|Yen bill with a representative drawing attributed to Takenouchi no Sukune on its obverse.]] *First convertible silver yen bill (1889-1958) *First five yen bill (1899-1939) *Third five yen bill (1916-1939) *Second one-yen bill (1943-1958) *Second 200 yen bill (1945-1946) ==Family== * '''Father:''' {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) * '''Mother:''' Yamashita no Kage-hime (影媛), sister of Kiinokuni no Miyatsukuko Uzuhiko (Ujihiko) (山下影日売) ** '''Wife(s):''' unknown *** '''Son:''' [[Hata no Yashiro]] (羽田矢代, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Hata clan (Ason)|Hata clan]] (波多氏) related to {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|WD=Q987290|lt=Hata clan}} (秦氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Kose no Okara]] (許勢小柄, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kose clan]] (巨勢氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Soga no Ishikawa]] (蘇我石川, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Soga clan]] (蘇我氏). *** '''Son:''' {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|WD=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tsuku}} (平群木菟, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Heguri clan]] (平群氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Ki no Tsuno]] (紀角, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ki clan]] (紀氏). *** '''Daughter:''' Kume no Matio-hime (久米能摩伊刀比売, ?–?) *** '''Daughter:''' Nonoiro-hime (怒能伊呂比売, ?–?) *** '''Son:''' [[Kazuragi no Sotsuhiko]] (葛城襲津彦, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Katsuragi clan]] (葛城氏). *** '''Son:''' Wakugo no Sukune (若子宿禰) ==Family tree== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Artwork== File:Empress Jingu and Minister Takenouchi by Utagawa Kunisada I.jpg|Empress Jingū (above) and Takenouchi no Sukune (below) Fishing at Chikuzen File:Takeuchi and the infant emperor.jpg|Minister Takeuchi carrying the infant {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}.
Made by [[Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] File:Empress Jingu and Takenouchi no Sukune Fishing at Chikuzen LACMA M.84.31.260.jpg|Empress Consort Jingū and Takenouchi fish in {{ill|Chikuzen Province |en|Chikuzen Province |WD=Q1047664|lt= Chikuzen}}.

[[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] {{Short description|Legendary Japanese hero-statesman}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Takenouchi no Sukune
{{lang|ja|武内宿禰}} | image = Takeshiuchi Sukune.jpg | office = ''[[Omi (title)|Ōomi]]'' | caption = Takenouchi no Sukune drawn by [[:en:Kikuchi Yosai|Kikuchi Yosai]] | birth_date = 84 AD | death_date = Unknown | children = {{Plainlist| * [[Hata no Yashiro]] * [[Kose no Okara]] * [[Soga no Ishikawa]] * Heguri no Tsuku * [[Ki no Tsuno]] * Kume no Matio-hime * Nonoiro-hime * [[Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko]] * Wakugo no Sukune}} | father = {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Takenouchi no Sukune'''|武内宿禰}} or '''Takeshiuchi no Sukune''' was a legendary Japanese hero-statesman of the 1st century, and a [[Shinto]] [[kami]]. He is recorded in Japan's earliest literary texts, the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (ca. {{ill|712|en|712|WD=Q23521|lt=712|1=712}}) and the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' ({{ill|720|en|720|WD=Q23539|lt=720|1=720}}). == Life == Takenouchi no Sukune was supposedly the son of Princess Kagehime, and is said to be grandson to {{Nihongo|Prince Hikofutsuoshinomakoto|彦太忍信命}}. Descended from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, Takenouchi no Sukune served under five legendary emperors, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, but was perhaps best known for his service as Grand Minister (Ōomi) to the Regent {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, with whom he supposedly invaded Korea. While Jingu was regent to her son, Ojin, Takenouchi was accused of treason. He underwent the "{{ill|Trial by ordeal|en|Trial by ordeal|WD=Q40745|lt=ordeal|1=Trial by ordeal}} of boiling water" as a way to prove his innocence. In addition to his martial services to these emperors, he was reputedly also a ''saniwa'', or {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=spirit medium|1=Oracle}}. He is said to have been the grandson of HIkofutsuoshinomakoto in the Nihon Shoki, where as the Kojiki states that he was the son of Hikofutsuoshinomakoto.{{Cite web |title=Takeuchi no Sukune • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/takeuchi-no-sukune/ |access-date=2022-11-02 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} == Possible connections to Korea == Japanese historians have theorized a possible inference to Takenouchi no Sukune found in ancient Korea's ''[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]]'' where a name called "Udojugun (于道朱君/우도주군)" is mentioned.{{Cite book |title=[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]] |chapter=Chapter 45 - Battle of Seok Uro}} In the book, it states that during the time a Japanese envoy "Galnago (葛那古/갈나고)" stayed in {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, a royal named "Seok Uro (昔于老/석우로)" accompanied him. However after a while, the two started to banter where Seok Uro threatened to "make the emperor of Japan a salt making slave and his wife who cooks rice" as a heavy joke. After relaying the message to the emperor himself (referred to as Emperor Ookimi[?]/大王/オオキミ), a general named Udojugun was sent to the Korean peninsula and declared war against Silla. According to scholars, Udojugun and Takenouchi no Sukune might have been the same individual as Takenouchi no Sukune's pronunciation would have been closer to "Utusukune (内宿禰)" in {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese|1=Old Japanese}}. In addition, ''{{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue|1=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'' also mentions Takenouchi no Sukune's accomplishments during the Silla-Wa War (新羅・倭戦争) further adding credence to his involvement during the Silla period.{{Cite book |title={{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue|1=Wakan Sansai Zue}} |volume=13 |chapter=異國人物 - 神功皇后征三韓}} As a similar sounding name appears in Korean records while also having historical documents that support his authenticity to an extent, scholars deduced that though not completely accurate, Takenouchi no Sukune can be considered an individual who had truly existed. According to world-renowned linguist and Japanese language expert {{ill|Alexander Vovin|en|Alexander Vovin|WD=Q848595|lt=Alexander Vovin|1=Alexander Vovin}}, due to [[Amenohiboko]] being of Korean origin, Empress Jingū and her son and successor, Emperor Ōjin might have been native speakers of the {{ill|Korean language|en|Korean language|WD=Q9176|lt=Korean language|1=Korean language}}.{{Cite book |last=Vovin |first=Alexander |title=Immigrants or Overlords? Korean Influences on Japan in the Archaic Period: a Linguistic Perspective |date=2012-06-14 |publisher=Institut für Kulturund Geitestesgeschichte Asiens |pages=29 |language=en}} Vovin states that since Takenouchi no Sukune was able to interact with Korean kingdoms during their rule, he stated that Takenouchi no Sukune may also have been of foreign origin as not only did he have jurisdiction over Japan-Korean affairs, but was also the father of many clan founders that have heavy connections with Korea. == Legacy == Twenty-eight Japanese clans are said to be descended from Takenouchi no Sukune, including {{ill|Takeuchi|en|Takeuchi|WD=Q23301551|lt=Takeuchi|1=Takeuchi}} and [[Soga clan|Soga]]. He is a legendary figure, and is said to have drunk daily from a sacred well, which helped him live to be 280 years old. He is enshrined as a [[Kami]] at the [[Ube Shrine|Ube shrine]], in {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=the Iwami district|1=Iwami District, Tottori}} of the {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} and at local [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman shrines]]. His portrait has appeared on the {{ill|Japanese yen|en|Japanese yen|WD=Q8146|lt=Japanese yen|1=Japanese yen}}, and dolls of him are popular [[:en:Kodomo no hi|Children's Day]] gifts. [[File:Takeuchi1JPY.JPG|thumb|Yen bill with a representative drawing attributed to Takenouchi no Sukune on its obverse.]] *First convertible silver yen bill (1889-1958) *First five yen bill (1899-1939) *Third five yen bill (1916-1939) *Second one-yen bill (1943-1958) *Second 200 yen bill (1945-1946) ==Family== * '''Father:''' {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) * '''Mother:''' Yamashita no Kage-hime (影媛), sister of Kiinokuni no Miyatsukuko Uzuhiko (Ujihiko) (山下影日売) ** '''Wife(s):''' unknown *** '''Son:''' [[Hata no Yashiro]] (羽田矢代, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Hata clan (Ason)|Hata clan]] (波多氏) related to {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|WD=Q987290|lt=Hata clan|1=Hata clan}} (秦氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Kose no Okara]] (許勢小柄, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kose clan]] (巨勢氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Soga no Ishikawa]] (蘇我石川, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Soga clan]] (蘇我氏). *** '''Son:''' {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|WD=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tsuku|1=Heguri no Tsuku}} (平群木菟, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Heguri clan]] (平群氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Ki no Tsuno]] (紀角, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ki clan]] (紀氏). *** '''Daughter:''' Kume no Matio-hime (久米能摩伊刀比売, ?–?) *** '''Daughter:''' Nonoiro-hime (怒能伊呂比売, ?–?) *** '''Son:''' [[Kazuragi no Sotsuhiko]] (葛城襲津彦, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Katsuragi clan]] (葛城氏). *** '''Son:''' Wakugo no Sukune (若子宿禰) ==Family tree== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Artwork== File:Empress Jingu and Minister Takenouchi by Utagawa Kunisada I.jpg|Empress Jingū (above) and Takenouchi no Sukune (below) Fishing at Chikuzen File:Takeuchi and the infant emperor.jpg|Minister Takeuchi carrying the infant {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}.

Print of Totoya Hokkei (1822) File:Daijin Takenouchino Sukune.jpg|Daijin Takenouchi no Sukune by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|1=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}} ==External links== * Encyclopedia of [[Shinto]]: [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=548 Biographical note] * {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|1=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}}: [http://www.sinister-designs.com/graphicarts/takenouchi2.html Ukiyo-e image (1883)] * Nippon Kindai Banknote:[http://kjp-banknote.zening.info/en/yen1a.htm Banknote portrait (1916)] * Takenouchi no Sukune Meets Dragon King of the Sea, Dallas Museum of Art, bronze sculpture:[https://web.archive.org/web/20110725215318/http://dallasmuseumofart.org:8080/emuseum/media/view/Objects/3145828/406?t:state:flow=e8345f50-b33b-4bf9-83c8-e226a8426160] ==Notes== {{notelist}} == References ==]] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{Hachiman Faith}} [[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{Commons category|Takenouchi no Sukune}} [[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]] [[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[ja:武内宿禰]] [[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]] [[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]] [[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[zh:武內宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q1073668}} [[Category:84 births]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Hachiman faith]] [[Category:Japanese mythology|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Shinto kami|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[co:commons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]][[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[ja:武内宿禰]][[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]][[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]][[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]][[zh:武內宿禰]] {{Short description|Legendary Japanese hero-statesman}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Takenouchi no Sukune
{{lang|ja|武内宿禰}} | image = Takeshiuchi Sukune.jpg | office = ''[[Omi (title)|Ōomi]]'' | caption = Takenouchi no Sukune drawn by [[:en:Kikuchi Yosai|Kikuchi Yosai]] | birth_date = 84 AD | death_date = Unknown | children = {{Plainlist| * [[Hata no Yashiro]] * [[Kose no Okara]] * [[Soga no Ishikawa]] * Heguri no Tsuku * [[Ki no Tsuno]] * Kume no Matio-hime * Nonoiro-hime * [[Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko]] * Wakugo no Sukune}} | father = {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Takenouchi no Sukune'''|武内宿禰}} or '''Takeshiuchi no Sukune''' was a legendary Japanese hero-statesman of the 1st century, and a [[Shinto]] [[kami]]. He is recorded in Japan's earliest literary texts, the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (ca. {{ill|712|en|712|WD=Q23521|lt=712|1=712}}) and the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' ({{ill|720|en|720|WD=Q23539|lt=720|1=720}}). == Life == Takenouchi no Sukune was supposedly the son of Princess Kagehime, and is said to be grandson to {{Nihongo|Prince Hikofutsuoshinomakoto|彦太忍信命}}. Descended from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, Takenouchi no Sukune served under five legendary emperors, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, but was perhaps best known for his service as Grand Minister (Ōomi) to the Regent {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, with whom he supposedly invaded Korea. While Jingu was regent to her son, Ojin, Takenouchi was accused of treason. He underwent the "{{ill|Trial by ordeal|en|Trial by ordeal|WD=Q40745|lt=ordeal|1=Trial by ordeal}} of boiling water" as a way to prove his innocence. In addition to his martial services to these emperors, he was reputedly also a ''saniwa'', or {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=spirit medium|1=Oracle}}. He is said to have been the grandson of HIkofutsuoshinomakoto in the Nihon Shoki, where as the Kojiki states that he was the son of Hikofutsuoshinomakoto.{{Cite web |title=Takeuchi no Sukune • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/takeuchi-no-sukune/ |access-date=2022-11-02 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} == Possible connections to Korea == Japanese historians have theorized a possible inference to Takenouchi no Sukune found in ancient Korea's ''[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]]'' where a name called "Udojugun (于道朱君/우도주군)" is mentioned.{{Cite book |title=[[:en:Samguk Sagi|Samguk Sagi]] |chapter=Chapter 45 - Battle of Seok Uro}} In the book, it states that during the time a Japanese envoy "Galnago (葛那古/갈나고)" stayed in {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, a royal named "Seok Uro (昔于老/석우로)" accompanied him. However after a while, the two started to banter where Seok Uro threatened to "make the emperor of Japan a salt making slave and his wife who cooks rice" as a heavy joke. After relaying the message to the emperor himself (referred to as Emperor Ookimi[?]/大王/オオキミ), a general named Udojugun was sent to the Korean peninsula and declared war against Silla. According to scholars, Udojugun and Takenouchi no Sukune might have been the same individual as Takenouchi no Sukune's pronunciation would have been closer to "Utusukune (内宿禰)" in {{ill|Old Japanese|en|Old Japanese|WD=Q5736700|lt=Old Japanese|1=Old Japanese}}. In addition, ''{{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue|1=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'' also mentions Takenouchi no Sukune's accomplishments during the Silla-Wa War (新羅・倭戦争) further adding credence to his involvement during the Silla period.{{Cite book |title={{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue|1=Wakan Sansai Zue}} |volume=13 |chapter=異國人物 - 神功皇后征三韓}} As a similar sounding name appears in Korean records while also having historical documents that support his authenticity to an extent, scholars deduced that though not completely accurate, Takenouchi no Sukune can be considered an individual who had truly existed. According to world-renowned linguist and Japanese language expert {{ill|Alexander Vovin|en|Alexander Vovin|WD=Q848595|lt=Alexander Vovin|1=Alexander Vovin}}, due to [[Amenohiboko]] being of Korean origin, Empress Jingū and her son and successor, Emperor Ōjin might have been native speakers of the {{ill|Korean language|en|Korean language|WD=Q9176|lt=Korean language|1=Korean language}}.{{Cite book |last=Vovin |first=Alexander |title=Immigrants or Overlords? Korean Influences on Japan in the Archaic Period: a Linguistic Perspective |date=2012-06-14 |publisher=Institut für Kulturund Geitestesgeschichte Asiens |pages=29 |language=en}} Vovin states that since Takenouchi no Sukune was able to interact with Korean kingdoms during their rule, he stated that Takenouchi no Sukune may also have been of foreign origin as not only did he have jurisdiction over Japan-Korean affairs, but was also the father of many clan founders that have heavy connections with Korea. == Legacy == Twenty-eight Japanese clans are said to be descended from Takenouchi no Sukune, including {{ill|Takeuchi|en|Takeuchi|WD=Q23301551|lt=Takeuchi|1=Takeuchi}} and [[Soga clan|Soga]]. He is a legendary figure, and is said to have drunk daily from a sacred well, which helped him live to be 280 years old. He is enshrined as a [[Kami]] at the [[Ube Shrine|Ube shrine]], in {{ill|Iwami District, Tottori|en|Iwami District, Tottori|WD=Q1131619|lt=the Iwami district|1=Iwami District, Tottori}} of the {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} and at local [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman shrines]]. His portrait has appeared on the {{ill|Japanese yen|en|Japanese yen|WD=Q8146|lt=Japanese yen|1=Japanese yen}}, and dolls of him are popular [[:en:Kodomo no hi|Children's Day]] gifts. [[File:Takeuchi1JPY.JPG|thumb|Yen bill with a representative drawing attributed to Takenouchi no Sukune on its obverse.]] *First convertible silver yen bill (1889-1958) *First five yen bill (1899-1939) *Third five yen bill (1916-1939) *Second one-yen bill (1943-1958) *Second 200 yen bill (1945-1946) ==Family== * '''Father:''' {{ill|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto|qq=}} (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) * '''Mother:''' Yamashita no Kage-hime (影媛), sister of Kiinokuni no Miyatsukuko Uzuhiko (Ujihiko) (山下影日売) ** '''Wife(s):''' unknown *** '''Son:''' [[Hata no Yashiro]] (羽田矢代, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Hata clan (Ason)|Hata clan]] (波多氏) related to {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|WD=Q987290|lt=Hata clan|1=Hata clan}} (秦氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Kose no Okara]] (許勢小柄, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kose clan]] (巨勢氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Soga no Ishikawa]] (蘇我石川, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Soga clan]] (蘇我氏). *** '''Son:''' {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|WD=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tsuku|1=Heguri no Tsuku}} (平群木菟, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Heguri clan]] (平群氏). *** '''Son:''' [[Ki no Tsuno]] (紀角, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ki clan]] (紀氏). *** '''Daughter:''' Kume no Matio-hime (久米能摩伊刀比売, ?–?) *** '''Daughter:''' Nonoiro-hime (怒能伊呂比売, ?–?) *** '''Son:''' [[Kazuragi no Sotsuhiko]] (葛城襲津彦, ?–?), ancestor of the [[Katsuragi clan]] (葛城氏). *** '''Son:''' Wakugo no Sukune (若子宿禰) ==Family tree== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Artwork== File:Empress Jingu and Minister Takenouchi by Utagawa Kunisada I.jpg|Empress Jingū (above) and Takenouchi no Sukune (below) Fishing at Chikuzen File:Takeuchi and the infant emperor.jpg|Minister Takeuchi carrying the infant {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}.
Made by [[Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] File:Empress Jingu and Takenouchi no Sukune Fishing at Chikuzen LACMA M.84.31.260.jpg|Empress Consort Jingū and Takenouchi fish in {{ill|Chikuzen Province |en|Chikuzen Province |WD=Q1047664|lt= Chikuzen|1=Chikuzen Province}}.
Print of Tsukioka Yoshitoshi (1876) File:De regent Takeuchi no Sukune-Rijksmuseum RP-P-1958-369.jpeg|The Regent Takeuchi no Sukune.
Print of Totoya Hokkei (1822) File:Daijin Takenouchino Sukune.jpg|Daijin Takenouchi no Sukune by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|1=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}} ==External links== * Encyclopedia of [[Shinto]]: [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=548 Biographical note] * {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|1=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}}: [http://www.sinister-designs.com/graphicarts/takenouchi2.html Ukiyo-e image (1883)] * Nippon Kindai Banknote:[http://kjp-banknote.zening.info/en/yen1a.htm Banknote portrait (1916)] * Takenouchi no Sukune Meets Dragon King of the Sea, Dallas Museum of Art, bronze sculpture:[https://web.archive.org/web/20110725215318/http://dallasmuseumofart.org:8080/emuseum/media/view/Objects/3145828/406?t:state:flow=e8345f50-b33b-4bf9-83c8-e226a8426160] ==Notes== {{notelist}} == References ==]] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{Hachiman Faith}} [[en:Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{Commons category|Takenouchi no Sukune}} [[de:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[es:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[fa:تاکنوشی نو سوکون]] [[fr:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[ja:武内宿禰]] [[ko:다케우치노 스쿠네]] [[mni:ꯇꯀꯦꯅꯣꯎꯆꯤ ꯅꯣ ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯦ]] [[pt:Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[zh:武內宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q1073668}} [[Category:84 births]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Hachiman faith]] [[Category:Japanese mythology|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Shinto kami|Takenouchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
